visa_types.overview:
Overview
This category of visa caters for people who are Married to Bulgarian Citizen.
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This category of visa caters for people who are Married to Bulgarian Citizen.
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.

## Visa Fees

[bgr > qat > en > Table:Business Visa > Visa Fees 1]

Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of QAR 140 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Document checklist for Married to Bulgarian Citizen (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2KkfmxYl9aTe0XmqTScEYG/fa8f25c6bf6b66e870c0ddf2dce99853/Document-Checklist-C-Type-Married-Bulgarian-Citizen.pdf) 

## To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.	
- It should be without border.
	
- It should have a light colored background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.

- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
	
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
	
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
	
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
	
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
	
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
	
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
	
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 3 months old.
	
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
	
- It should be without hat/cap.
	
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
	
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
	
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
	
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
	
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
	
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
	
- Scanned & color photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Frequently Asked Questions
- If you want to visit the Republic of Bulgaria, at which Embassy do
                                        I apply for a visa?
You must apply at the Embassy / Consulate of the Republic
                                        of Bulgaria in the country you are resident. If you are planning to visit
                                        several countries, you need to apply at all the respective Embassies 
- Can I just come to the Embassy / Consulate of the Republic of
                                        Bulgaria and apply right away? 
No, you will need to submit the application to the Visa
                                        Application Center in your country or can schedule an appointment first in order
                                        to submit your visa application by calling the Bulgaria Visa Application
                                        Centre(more information on the web site https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/bgr/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/bgr/)
- Where is the Visa Application Centre located? 
The new Visa Application Centre is located at Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/sau/en/bgr/attend-centre)
- Where Do I pay the Visa Fees? 
After submission of the visa application at the visa
                                        application center an applicant pays a service charge and the Visa Fee directly
                                        at the Visa Application Centre. Please note that Visa Fee is in and is decided
                                        by the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria.
- Do I have to apply personally? 
Applications can only be submitted in person
- Can my passport be collected by someone else? 
Application/Passport can be collected either by the
                                        applicant in person or through an authorized representative that has an
                                        authority letter 
- Can I live in Bulgaria having a short term visa?
 No, short term visa enables you to stay in Bulgaria for
                                        the period of its validity only. According to the law in the area of rights and
                                        matters of foreigners in Bulgaria, in order to settle in Bulgaria, you must have
                                        a necesQARy settlement or National Bulgaria visa. Please contact the Bulgaria
                                        Embassy/ Consulate directly for more information or you can also visit the
                                        Important Links section 
- Do I need to have a medical insurance?
Yes. Medical insurance is an important part of the
                                        dossier.
- What do I do if I am refused? 
If you consider it to be reasonable, you can appeal
                                        against the refusal. For more information regarding appeals, please contact the
                                        Bulgaria Embassy / consulate. 
- If I am refused, should I wait for some time before applying for a
                                        visa again?
No. The Embassy recommends you to apply again only if you
                                        think that the circumstances that could have caused the refusal have changed.

## Processing Time
Visa processing time can take up to 15 working days.

## Application Form

 
Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3bipw88kNA6ntKqAZOW3OX/da430da9fa354c5bfd3b4a8d3c27c6a7/application-form-2024.pdf) 

## To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.visa_fees:
[bgr > qat > en > Table:Business Visa > Visa Fees 1]

Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of QAR 140 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time: Visa processing time can take up to 15 working days.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A tourist visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Bulgaria for a
                                    limited period of time. This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit
                                    Bulgaria for touristic purposes either through buying a tour via an accredited
                                    travel agency, or through self-bookings of tickets and hotel.
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A business visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Bulgaria for
                                    a limited period of time. This category of visa caters for people wishing to visit
                                    Bulgaria, because they maintain an active relationship with a Bulgarian company, are
                                    establishing new contracts, are going to participate in a professional training.
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A seasonal work visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Bulgaria
                                    for a limited period of time. This category of visa caters for people who are
                                    temporarily employed in the Republic of Bulgaria for a period up to 90 days. 
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Bulgaria for a limited
                                    period of time. This category of visa caters for people wishing visit a member of
                                    his/her family or friends, who are residents in Bulgaria.
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Bulgaria for a limited
                                    period of time. This category of visa caters for people wishing visit a member of
                                    his/her family or friends, who are residents in Bulgaria.
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.

## Visa Fees

[bgr > qat > en > Table:Business Visa > Visa Fees 1]

Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of QAR 140 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Documents Checklist required for  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6zQrBjf00SIuuRiXBoazQL/48a2e68565e1b24fdec56a347d5257ca/Document-Checklist-C-Type-Private-visit.pdf)PRIVATE VISIT (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6zQrBjf00SIuuRiXBoazQL/48a2e68565e1b24fdec56a347d5257ca/Document-Checklist-C-Type-Private-visit.pdf) 

## To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.	
- It should be without border.
	
- It should have a light colored background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.

- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
	
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
	
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
	
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
	
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
	
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
	
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
	
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 3 months old.
	
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
	
- It should be without hat/cap.
	
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
	
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
	
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
	
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
	
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
	
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
	
- Scanned & color photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Frequently Asked Questions
- If you want to visit the Republic of Bulgaria, at which Embassy do
                                        I apply for a visa?
You must apply at the Embassy / Consulate of the Republic
                                        of Bulgaria in the country you are resident. If you are planning to visit
                                        several countries, you need to apply at all the respective Embassies 
- Can I just come to the Embassy / Consulate of the Republic of
                                        Bulgaria and apply right away? 
No, you will need to submit the application to the Visa
                                        Application Center in your country or can schedule an appointment first in order
                                        to submit your visa application by calling the Bulgaria Visa Application
                                        Centre(more information on the web site https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/bgr/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/bgr/)
- Where is the Visa Application Centre located? 
The new Visa Application Centre is located at Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/sau/en/bgr/attend-centre)
- Where Do I pay the Visa Fees? 
After submission of the visa application at the visa
                                        application center an applicant pays a service charge and the Visa Fee directly
                                        at the Visa Application Centre. Please note that Visa Fee is in and is decided
                                        by the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria.
- Do I have to apply personally? 
Applications can only be submitted in person
- Can my passport be collected by someone else? 
Application/Passport can be collected either by the
                                        applicant in person or through an authorized representative that has an
                                        authority letter 
- Can I live in Bulgaria having a short term visa?
 No, short term visa enables you to stay in Bulgaria for
                                        the period of its validity only. According to the law in the area of rights and
                                        matters of foreigners in Bulgaria, in order to settle in Bulgaria, you must have
                                        a necesQARy settlement or National Bulgaria visa. Please contact the Bulgaria
                                        Embassy/ Consulate directly for more information or you can also visit the
                                        Important Links section 
- Do I need to have a medical insurance?
Yes. Medical insurance is an important part of the
                                        dossier.
- What do I do if I am refused? 
If you consider it to be reasonable, you can appeal
                                        against the refusal. For more information regarding appeals, please contact the
                                        Bulgaria Embassy / consulate. 
- If I am refused, should I wait for some time before applying for a
                                        visa again?
No. The Embassy recommends you to apply again only if you
                                        think that the circumstances that could have caused the refusal have changed.

## Processing Time
Visa processing time can take up to 15 working days.

## Application Form

 
Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3bipw88kNA6ntKqAZOW3OX/da430da9fa354c5bfd3b4a8d3c27c6a7/application-form-2024.pdf) 

## To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

## Overview
This category of visa caters for people wishing visit Bulgaria to participate in a conference, Training, sporting or cultural event or other similar activities.
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.

## Visa Fees

[bgr > qat > en > Table:Business Visa > Visa Fees 1]

Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of QAR 140 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required

- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.	
- It should be without border.
	
- It should have a light colored background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.

- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
	
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
	
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
	
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
	
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
	
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
	
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
	
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 3 months old.
	
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
	
- It should be without hat/cap.
	
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
	
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
	
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
	
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
	
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
	
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
	
- Scanned & color photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Frequently Asked Questions
- If you want to visit the Republic of Bulgaria, at which Embassy do
                                        I apply for a visa?
You must apply at the Embassy / Consulate of the Republic
                                        of Bulgaria in the country you are resident. If you are planning to visit
                                        several countries, you need to apply at all the respective Embassies 
- Can I just come to the Embassy / Consulate of the Republic of
                                        Bulgaria and apply right away? 
No, you will need to submit the application to the Visa
                                        Application Center in your country or can schedule an appointment first in order
                                        to submit your visa application by calling the Bulgaria Visa Application
                                        Centre(more information on the web site https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/bgr/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/bgr/)
- Where is the Visa Application Centre located? 
The new Visa Application Centre is located at Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/sau/en/bgr/attend-centre)
- Where Do I pay the Visa Fees? 
After submission of the visa application at the visa
                                        application center an applicant pays a service charge and the Visa Fee directly
                                        at the Visa Application Centre. Please note that Visa Fee is in and is decided
                                        by the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria.
- Do I have to apply personally? 
Applications can only be submitted in person
- Can my passport be collected by someone else? 
Application/Passport can be collected either by the
                                        applicant in person or through an authorized representative that has an
                                        authority letter 
- Can I live in Bulgaria having a short term visa?
 No, short term visa enables you to stay in Bulgaria for
                                        the period of its validity only. According to the law in the area of rights and
                                        matters of foreigners in Bulgaria, in order to settle in Bulgaria, you must have
                                        a necesQARy settlement or National Bulgaria visa. Please contact the Bulgaria
                                        Embassy/ Consulate directly for more information or you can also visit the
                                        Important Links section 
- Do I need to have a medical insurance?
Yes. Medical insurance is an important part of the
                                        dossier.
- What do I do if I am refused? 
If you consider it to be reasonable, you can appeal
                                        against the refusal. For more information regarding appeals, please contact the
                                        Bulgaria Embassy / consulate. 
- If I am refused, should I wait for some time before applying for a
                                        visa again?
No. The Embassy recommends you to apply again only if you
                                        think that the circumstances that could have caused the refusal have changed.

visa_types.name: Long Term D Category Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
The list of documents below is not an exhaustive list of documents and the
                                    applicant can always be asked to provide additional information/documents, if
                                    desired by the Embassy.
Both the standard and the supporting documentation have to be recent, i.e. not
                                    older than 1 month, and need to be provided for every Long-term visa application.
                                  
Applicants always have to bring and present their original documents, but they only
                                    need to submit photocopies of the same; unless the requirements specify that an
                                    original document has
                                    to be submitted.

Documents Checklist required for Long Term D visa category (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7Bo2jphqlZM2O0YvuNZ8zH/86a93fabad749aae318408d388e664d5/Document-Checklist-Long-Term-D-student-visa.pdf)

- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of QAR 140 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.

The minimum processing time of a visa application is 15 calendar days, so
                                    applicants are advised to leave enough time - between the appointment date and
                                    travel date - for the Embassy
                                    to process their visa application; no guarantees can be given on the visa
                                    application’s return date as delays may occur.
For certain nationalities (see list here (https://www.mfa.gov.lv/en/consular-information/prior-consulation)) the visa application needs consultation
                                    from the other Schengen countries and therefore the minimum processing time takes 15
                                    working days.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that
                                    the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the
                                    Embassy’s requirements (see
                                    Documents Required for the different visa categories).

Overview

                                    for a Long-term D Category Visa.
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you want to work or study in Bulgaria for longer than 90 days, you should apply
                                    for a Long-term D Category Visa.
Please note that you are only allowed to apply for Schengen Visa in Bulgaria as early as 90 days before the expected date of departure.

## Visa Fees

[bgr > qat > en > Table:Long Term D Category Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of QAR 140 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
The list of documents below is not an exhaustive list of documents and the
                                    applicant can always be asked to provide additional information/documents, if
                                    desired by the Embassy.
Both the standard and the supporting documentation have to be recent, i.e. not
                                    older than 1 month, and need to be provided for every Long-term visa application.
                                  
Applicants always have to bring and present their original documents, but they only
                                    need to submit photocopies of the same; unless the requirements specify that an
                                    original document has
                                    to be submitted.

Documents Checklist required for Long Term D visa category (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7Bo2jphqlZM2O0YvuNZ8zH/86a93fabad749aae318408d388e664d5/Document-Checklist-Long-Term-D-student-visa.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
 Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of
                                    yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are
                                      distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless
                                      the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic
                                      background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (paper clipped) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa
                                    application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new
                                    photographs in the photo booth
                                    in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
 The minimum processing time of a visa application is 15 calendar days, so
                                    applicants are advised to leave enough time - between the appointment date and
                                    travel date - for the Embassy
                                    to process their visa application; no guarantees can be given on the visa
                                    application’s return date as delays may occur.
For certain nationalities (see list here (https://www.mfa.gov.lv/en/consular-information/prior-consulation)) the visa application needs consultation
                                    from the other Schengen countries and therefore the minimum processing time takes 15
                                    working days.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that
                                    the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the
                                    Embassy’s requirements (see
                                    Documents Required for the different visa categories).

## Online Application Form
- Online Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1Aoyh3ndFA7YVAMUzS1ETK/d21d2d0fb69b57bb424c660dec596c73/application-form-2024.pdf)
